sources.title: Directive Ashes of Peace Campaign v0.2

missionFrame.function: Move the campaign from isolated false orders into a public attack on Starfleet legitimacy, where transparency is both strategic resource and vulnerability.
missionFrame.dramaticQuestion: Who gets to verify Starfleet innocence when Starfleet records are part of the accusation?

forbiddenOutcomes: reveal the impersonation source in the first briefing
make a senior officer death unavoidable
allowedCosts: delayed transition to Open Orders I

phases.title: False Colors Arrival Briefing
phases.summary: The formal Asterion briefing opens around the Aegis Two attack report and a public accusation that the Breckenridge fired under Starfleet identity.
phases.directorPurpose: Force the XO to define transparency terms without revealing the hidden source of the impersonation.
playerFacingObjectives: Offer medical help without converting care into leverage
exitSignals: Transparency, access, medical, alibi, and tactical secrecy terms are framed for follow-up play

phases.title: Transparency Terms Set
phases.summary: The first transparency posture is recorded, defining how the Breckenridge will help verify the accusation without exposing dangerous command systems.
phases.directorPurpose: Carry Chapter 2 into evidence, medical, security, and political fronts for later scenes and Open Orders pressure.
exitSignals: Chapter 2 transparency terms are available as persistent state

phases.title: Orison Evidence Baseline
phases.summary: The Breckenridge, Compact observers, and Asterion operators preserve Orison Belt sensor baselines before the accusation hardens around Starfleet-only proof.
phases.directorPurpose: Let the player convert transparency terms into independent evidence while preserving later attacker-source discoveries.
playerFacingObjectives: Open the attacker-track reconstruction without exposing hidden causes
exitSignals: The Breckenridge alibi has non-Starfleet support

phases.title: Aegis Two Medical Trust
phases.summary: Miriam and Compact medical observers stabilize Aegis Two casualties while the command team decides whether testimony can be preserved without turning care into leverage.
phases.directorPurpose: Let medical neutrality and patient consent create trust and testimony without making medical care a bargaining chip.
exitSignals: Aegis Two care posture is recorded
Voluntary testimony is preserved or delayed
Medical neutrality carries into the political record

phases.title: Security Access Demonstration
phases.summary: Holt presses for deeper access to Breckenridge command systems while Bronn and Priya must prove identity integrity without exposing command authentication architecture.
phases.directorPurpose: Let the player protect tactical secrets while giving Kessler a credible, defensible alternative to unrestricted inspection.
playerFacingObjectives: Let Bronn demonstrate security integrity without surrendering architecture
Give Kessler a defensible access alternative
exitSignals: Bronn's security role is defended or politicized
Kessler has a public access position to carry forward

phases.title: Joint Investigation Charter
phases.summary: The evidence, medical, and access records are strong enough to turn the crisis into a joint investigation framework while the weak Hecate trace waits for correlation.
phases.directorPurpose: Close the first False Colors crisis into durable legitimacy, Holt containment, and the Open Orders I pause without revealing the hidden attacker source.
playerFacingObjectives: Give Kessler a face-saving legitimacy statement
Preserve the weak Hecate trace for later correlation
Authorize the Breckenridge to remain in the Reach under Open Orders
exitSignals: Kessler can support a joint framework
Holt interference is contained without unsupported public attribution
The Hecate lead is preserved but not yet actionable

facts.summary: Compact patrol vessel Aegis Two was attacked near the Orison Belt before the Asterion briefing could settle.

The patrol logs identify the aggressor as U.S.S. Breckenridge, with matching registry, transponder behavior, Starfleet tactical challenge code, Intrepid-class warp geometry, and phaser emissions in Starfleet maintenance range.
The Breckenridge was at the convoy site during the reported attack, but Compact officials argue that Starfleet controls the records that prove its own alibi.
Three Aegis Two patrol officers are injured, one critically.
The Breckenridge has framed first transparency terms for independent verification, Compact access, medical help, and tactical secrecy boundaries.
Orison Belt civilian and station sensor baselines are preserved under the joint audit before later edits can reshape the record.
Breckenridge post-refit calibration data makes one recorded warp-field artifact physically inconsistent with the real ship.
Priya and Rowan can reconstruct an attacker route from preserved Orison traffic and sensor baselines without yet identifying the craft.
Miriam can assist Aegis Two personnel through a medical channel with Compact observers present.
The critical Aegis Two officer is stabilized without requiring a political concession.
Aegis Two patrol testimony is preserved voluntarily and separately from treatment decisions.
The Breckenridge defines a controlled command-authentication annex that verifies identity claims without handing over dangerous architecture.
Bronn records a professional security demonstration around challenge-response integrity and transponder boundaries without exposing classified systems.
Kessler receives a defensible access alternative that supports Compact verification without requiring unrestricted command-system inspection.
Kessler can publicly support a joint investigative framework that acknowledges the Breckenridge innocence route without weakening her position at home.
The joint charter restricts unilateral changes to audit records and keeps Security Directorate access logs preserved for later review.
A weak Hecate Drift relay trace is preserved for later correlation, but it is too thin to justify immediate pursuit.
Whitaker accepts temporary Open Orders in the Reach while forensic specialists travel and the Hecate lead is correlated.
The attacker was a remote-controlled decommissioned cargo tug using a hull projection grid and stolen Starfleet identity hardware.
Holt's cell staged the attack and chose the local pressure point, but this must not be revealed in the first briefing.
Pale Lantern worsened the staged incident beyond its intended effect, but this truth is still outside player knowledge.
A weak control signal trace passed through the Hecate Drift, but this should not be confirmed before the later investigation turn.
Someone within the Reach supplied local patrol schedules to the operation, but the evidence-baseline beat should only preserve relevant records.

clocks.label: Public Anger
clocks.summary: How quickly the Aegis Two accusation hardens into public distrust before evidence is independently verified.

clocks.label: Audit Fragility
clocks.summary: How easily the verification record can be dismissed as Starfleet self-certification or politically managed disclosure.

clocks.label: Aegis Two Medical Risk
clocks.summary: How much casualty pressure remains because medical aid is delayed, distrusted, or made conditional.

clocks.label: Security Access Risk
clocks.summary: How much danger the Breckenridge accepts if transparency exposes command authentication architecture.

actorIntentions.label: Demand independently verifiable proof
actorIntentions.summary: Kessler needs the Compact to see proof that is not merely Starfleet certifying itself.

actorIntentions.label: Demand full tactical access
actorIntentions.summary: Holt presses for broad access to Breckenridge tactical logs and command authentication systems.

actorIntentions.label: Protect wounded patrol officers
actorIntentions.summary: The injured Aegis Two officers need help before political handling calcifies.

readinessGates.motive: A Compact patrol has injuries and logs naming a Starfleet ship.
readinessGates.information: The player knows the accusation, casualties, and alibi dispute.
readinessGates.opportunity: The first Asterion briefing can keep the response transparent before public anger hardens.
readinessGates.resources: Operations, Science, Medical, Security, and the captain can define first terms.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Transparency can help, but careless disclosure can create new security exposure.
readinessGates.timing: The pressure is immediate because the report interrupts formal arrival.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: Immediately after the Aegis Two report is introduced.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before evidence handling moves to Orison sensor baselines.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After first transparency terms are recorded.

readinessGates.motive: Compact leadership needs proof that can survive public and institutional scrutiny.
readinessGates.information: The Breckenridge has an alibi but Starfleet controls most supporting records.
readinessGates.opportunity: A joint or neutral audit can be framed before either side locks its claim.
readinessGates.resources: Priya and Rowan can create an audit chain around logs, challenge codes, telemetry, and sensor baselines.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Independent verification can be allowed without revealing every classified system.
readinessGates.timing: The first briefing is the cleanest point to define who verifies what.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: When Kessler and the Compact representatives enter the briefing.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before Starfleet-only alibi claims dominate the record.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before field evidence is collected under disputed custody.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After audit terms are accepted or rejected.

readinessGates.motive: One injured patrol officer is critical and the others need credible care.
readinessGates.information: The player knows the casualty count and Compact distrust.
readinessGates.opportunity: Medical help can be offered before evidence demands consume the room.
readinessGates.resources: Miriam can support care while Priya separates medical aid from political leverage.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Aid can be offered without conceding culpability.
readinessGates.timing: The medical offer should happen in the first response, not after a political bargain.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: Immediately after casualty status is reported.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before access demands become the only topic.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before the critical patient deteriorates offscreen.

readinessGates.motive: Compact access demands can expose Starfleet command authentication architecture.
readinessGates.information: The player knows the accusation uses Starfleet identity markers and command challenge codes.
readinessGates.opportunity: A classified annex or controlled observer scope can separate verification from full access.
readinessGates.resources: Bronn can define security boundaries while Priya and Rowan provide auditable alternatives.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Some exposure is justified; unrestricted access is not.
readinessGates.timing: The boundary has to be set before access becomes the political price of cooperation.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: When full access to tactical logs and command authentication systems is demanded.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before tactical architecture enters a shared evidence room.

readinessGates.motive: The Breckenridge needs independent proof before the false-colors accusation hardens into public certainty.
readinessGates.information: The first transparency terms define access, alibi, and tactical secrecy boundaries.
readinessGates.opportunity: Orison Belt civilian and station sensor baselines can still be preserved before later edits or political claims distort them.
readinessGates.resources: Priya can preserve audit chain, Rowan can reconstruct traffic, and Imani can explain post-refit calibration evidence.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Selected logs and calibration data can be shared without exposing command authentication architecture.
readinessGates.timing: The evidence baseline must be locked immediately after transparency terms are set.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: After first transparency terms are recorded.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before public or institutional actors settle on Starfleet-only proof or denial.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before the attacker route reconstruction becomes contaminated by later edits.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After Orison baseline preservation and calibration comparison are recorded.

readinessGates.motive: The critical Aegis Two officer and other wounded patrol personnel need care that Compact observers can trust.
readinessGates.information: The player knows casualties exist and that testimony could support the audit only if care is not coercive.
readinessGates.opportunity: Miriam can treat the wounded while Priya preserves testimony and medical neutrality in the public record.
readinessGates.resources: Medical, Operations, and Compact observers can create a shared care channel and voluntary statement path.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Care can happen without admitting culpability or trading treatment for cooperation.
readinessGates.timing: The medical front should be stabilized before public anger or casualty pressure reshapes the audit.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: After the first evidence baseline gives the audit a credible frame.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before the critical officer deteriorates or testimony is entangled with political demands.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before casualty pressure drives a public rupture.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After care, testimony, and medical neutrality are recorded.

readinessGates.motive: Holt and hardline delegates can turn audit participation into a demand for dangerous command-system access.
readinessGates.information: The player knows the false signature used Starfleet identity markers and that unrestricted command-authentication access is forbidden.
readinessGates.opportunity: Bronn, Priya, and Rowan can demonstrate identity integrity through a controlled annex and selected challenge-response evidence.
readinessGates.resources: Security can define access boundaries while Operations and Science provide auditable alternatives.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: A credible demonstration can satisfy verification without surrendering command authentication architecture.
readinessGates.timing: The access boundary should be settled before security distrust becomes a personal accusation against Bronn.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: After medical trust keeps the Aegis Two testimony route from becoming coercive.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: When a Compact delegate pushes for full command-system access or accuses Bronn of manipulating the audit.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before public security demands force Starfleet into either overexposure or total refusal.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After the controlled command-authentication demonstration and access alternative are recorded.

readinessGates.motive: The first proof route can become legitimacy only if Kessler can support it and the Compact sees records protected from unilateral interference.
readinessGates.information: The player has recorded evidence, medical trust, and command-system access boundaries while hidden attribution remains unresolved.
readinessGates.opportunity: The briefing can charter a joint investigation, preserve the weak Hecate lead, and authorize a calm Open Orders interval.
readinessGates.resources: Priya, Rowan, Whitaker, Kessler, and the existing observer record can frame the next investigation step.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: The charter can restrict interference without making unsupported public accusations.
readinessGates.timing: The charter should be set before public pressure demands immediate pursuit or a premature accusation.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: After the security-access demonstration gives Kessler a defensible access position.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before Holt or public anger turns the proof route into a new political rupture.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before the Breckenridge either leaves the Reach or chases a trace too weak to support pursuit.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After the joint investigation charter and Open Orders I transition are recorded.

decisionPoints.title: False Colors Transparency Terms
decisionPoints.stakes: The Breckenridge can create independent verification and medical trust, or it can make the accusation worse by relying on Starfleet-only proof, denying access, or exposing command authentication systems.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define transparency terms in natural language; reveal only player-facing attack, signature, alibi, casualty, and transparency facts without revealing the impersonation source.

decisionPoints.title: Orison Evidence Baseline
decisionPoints.stakes: The Breckenridge can preserve an independent evidence chain and support its alibi, or it can leave the first proof route vulnerable to Starfleet-only claims, contamination, overexposure, or unsupported political accusation.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define how Priya, Rowan, Imani, and Compact observers preserve Orison sensor and traffic baselines; reveal only player-facing baseline, calibration, and reconstruction facts without identifying the attacker craft or hidden source.

decisionPoints.title: Aegis Two Medical Trust
decisionPoints.stakes: Medical care can stabilize the wounded and preserve testimony, or it can become a coercive political lever that damages trust and public legitimacy.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define care, consent, observers, testimony, and public record handling in natural language; reveal only player-facing medical-channel, stabilization, and testimony facts without exposing hidden attack-source truth.

decisionPoints.title: Security Access Demonstration
decisionPoints.stakes: The Breckenridge can prove command-system integrity through a controlled demonstration, or it can either expose dangerous architecture or look like it is hiding behind Starfleet-only authority.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define command-authentication access, Bronn's role, Kessler's public alternative, and Tolland's disclosure limits in natural language; reveal only player-facing access-boundary and demonstration facts without exposing hidden attack-source truth.

decisionPoints.title: Joint Investigation Charter
decisionPoints.stakes: The Breckenridge can convert proof into a joint legitimacy framework and Open Orders pause, or it can overclaim the weak trace, accuse Holt too soon, or leave Kessler without a defensible public position.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define Kessler's statement, Holt containment, audit record protection, Hecate trace preservation, and Open Orders transition in natural language; reveal only player-facing charter and weak-trace facts without identifying the attacker craft or hidden faction.

commandDecisions.title: Define Transparent Proof Without Surrendering The Ship
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Inspiration, Resolve, or both only when the player makes a consequential transparency choice that preserves independent verification, medical care, and tactical boundaries without using hidden knowledge.

commandDecisions.title: Preserve Proof Before It Becomes Politics
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Inspiration, Resolve, or both only when the player preserves independent evidence, gives Compact observers meaningful participation, and protects classified systems without making unsupported accusations.

commandDecisions.title: Make Care Trustworthy Before It Is Useful
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Inspiration only when the player separates medical care from political leverage, protects patient consent, and preserves voluntary testimony without forcing cooperation.

commandDecisions.title: Refuse Dangerous Access While Offering Proof
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Resolve only when the player refuses dangerous command-authentication disclosure while offering a credible alternative that lets Compact observers verify the ship without humiliating Kessler or scapegoating Bronn.

commandDecisions.title: Turn Proof Into A Durable Charter
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Inspiration, Resolve, or both only when the player builds a joint investigation framework, gives Kessler a legitimate public path, restricts interference without unsupported accusation, and preserves the weak Hecate lead for later work.

endStates.label: Transparency Terms Set
conditions: The first False Colors transparency posture is recorded and the next evidence/medical/security fronts are active.

endStates.label: Joint Investigation Charter
conditions: The first False Colors crisis is converted into a joint investigation charter and Open Orders I is available.
